summary: Iteration 258 — Verified the Zoho 'uncategorized bank-feed lines' import fix end-to-end. zoho_service now has fetch_bank_accounts() (GET /bankaccounts) and fetch_uncategorized_bank_transactions(account_id, …) (GET /banktransactions/uncategorized); routes/accounting_transactions._run_sync pulls BOTH the register source AND the per-account uncategorized source, merges them (dedup by Zoho id), enforces the date window client-side, skips non-bank/credit_card accounts, enriches account_name from the parent account, and records non-fatal failures in job.warnings without aborting the register pull. 10/10 pytest pass.
action_items: None — the production fix is fully verified in this preview env. Safe to redeploy. After redeploy, run a real Zoho sync for the affected month and confirm (a) BOTH the 53 register rows AND the uncategorized statement lines now land in accounting_transactions, (b) the job ends status='completed' with job.new reflecting the combined total, and (c) only the requested month's rows appear in the inbox.
critical_code_review_comments: services/zoho_service.py:3131-3138 — fetch_bank_accounts hits GET /books/v3/bankaccounts with per_page=200; correctly handles both 'bankaccounts' and 'bank_accounts' keys in the response. Good.

services/zoho_service.py:3141-3162 — fetch_uncategorized_bank_transactions correctly sends account_id + page + per_page (and date_start/date_end when given) to /books/v3/banktransactions/uncategorized. account_id is mandatory and is the FIRST param after tenant_id — Zoho rejects this endpoint without an account_id. Verified via monkeypatch capture.

routes/accounting_transactions.py:182-198 — _norm_filter() enriches uncategorized lines with account_id/account_name from the parent account before normalising (setdefault — does NOT overwrite if the line already carries its own account fields). Window filter applied INSIDE the page loop so out-of-range rows never reach the DB.

routes/accounting_transactions.py:265-310 — _run_sync's _drain() helper cleanly factors the page loop. Defensive try/except around fetch_bank_accounts AND each per-account uncategorized pull — both append a human-readable warning to job.warnings, the register pull is NEVER aborted by an uncategorized failure. Non-bank account types ('other_current_asset', etc.) are skipped before the per-account fetch. Solid design.

Minor (non-blocking): the per-account uncategorized fetcher is sequential (one account at a time). For tenants with many bank accounts this could add up. asyncio.gather over the accounts would be faster, but at the cost of harder error attribution. Skip unless prod sync times become a problem.

Minor (non-blocking): _drain() passes `label` to the progress doc but the job.progress doesn't keep a running per-source breakdown — once 'uncategorized' starts, the 'register' page count is overwritten. Acceptable for now since job.new / job.updated are cumulative.
test_credentials: CEO: surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth / test123 (login returns session_token → Authorization: Bearer <session_token>) — same as iter 257.
seed_data_creation: Three integration tests each insert ≤3 rows into accounting_transactions + 1 row into accounting_txn_sync_jobs + 1 counter, all under a uuid-suffixed TEST_tenant_* tenant. Each test deletes its own docs in finally (counters included). Real inbox not polluted — verified counters/accounting_transactions docs all carry the TEST_tenant_* tenant_id.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Zoho is NOT connected in this preview tenant — all Zoho calls (fetch_bank_transactions, fetch_bank_accounts, fetch_uncategorized_bank_transactions, _zoho_request, get_credentials) are monkeypatched. The test file uses @pytest.mark.asyncio(loop_scope='module') so all async tests share a single motor event loop — DO NOT remove that scope or the 2nd+ integration tests will fail with 'Event loop is closed'. To validate against live Zoho after redeploy, trigger /sync for the affected month and verify (a) sum of register + uncategorized matches user's bank statement (not just the 53 categorized rows), (b) job.warnings empty, (c) every row's date inside the requested window.
rca of the issue: Prod 'only 53 transactions imported' bug RCA: Zoho Books separates 'register transactions' (GET /banktransactions — categorized/matched/manually-added/excluded) from 'uncategorized statement lines' (a SEPARATE resource GET /banktransactions/uncategorized?account_id=...). The register endpoint NEVER returns uncategorized lines regardless of status param. Earlier sync only called /banktransactions, so it pulled only the 53 already-categorized rows; the much larger uncategorized statement-line population was invisible. FIX: _run_sync now (1) calls fetch_bank_transactions (register source), (2) calls fetch_bank_accounts to enumerate bank/credit_card accounts, (3) for each such account calls fetch_uncategorized_bank_transactions and merges results, (4) dedupes by zoho_transaction_id via the existing unique index, (5) client-side window-filters via _date_in_range. Verified via 10 pytest covering all 6 review-request bullets (integration merge, date window, account-type skip, account enrichment, two defensive failure modes, register-only-on-uncategorized-failure, backend regression of /sync 400 + list/flow-summary/export 2xx).
